title: Aleuritopteris tenella
taxonomy: Aleuritopteris tenella belongs to the family Pteridaceae, commonly known as the maidenhair fern family. It is a species of fern in the genus Aleuritopteris. Aleuritopteris tenella is a type of terrestrial fern, typically found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Aleuritopteris tenella is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Pteridophyta, and class Polypodiopsida. This fern species is known for its delicate fronds and is often found in shaded areas with high humidity.

The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and ease pain.
